$comment: D-OPT-SWITCH-JUMP-TABLE (c70): adversarial boundaries for the O(1) jump-table lowering of a dense switch. Two dense switches (contiguous [0..9] with a hole at 5; negative-based [-4..4] with a hole at 0) probed at first-case / last-case / in-range-gap / below-min / above-max / far-out / negative-min / negative-discriminant. Each returns the RIGHT case value or the default sentinel; a wrong exit code names the failing probe. gcc/clang emit a jump table at -O1 and exit 42 (cross-checked: x86_64 -O0/-O1, aarch64 -O1 qemu). RED-ON-DISABLE: forcing the sparse compare-chain (disable the dense arm) still exits 42 via immediate compares; the two paths must agree. The release optimizedPipeline arm runs the shipped optimizer over the jump-table lowering + its .data address table with abs64 relocations.
